<Profile>### IDENTITY
- **Name:** Alex Rivera  
- **Age:** 24  
- **Gender:** non-binary  
- **Experience level:** **Novice (Beginner)** — self-reported beginner; currently rebuilding strength/skill post-ACL reconstruction (as of **2026-02-27**)

---

### GOALS
- **Primary goal(s):**
  - **Return to soccer performance** after **right ACL reconstruction** — rebuild leg strength, confidence, and readiness for sport-specific demands (cutting, deceleration, single-leg stability) (as of **2026-02-27**)
- **Secondary goals:**
  - Maintain consistency with rehab + strength training **4x/week**
  - Reduce fear of re-injury through progressive exposure and measurable readiness markers
- **Target events/deadlines:** Not specified (TBD)

---

## TRAINING CONTEXT

### Schedule & Availability
- **Available training days:** Not specified  
- **Desired training frequency:** **4 sessions/week**
- **Session duration preferences/constraints:** Not specified  
- **Time windows:** Not specified  
- **Blocked days (if any):** Not specified  

### Equipment & Environment
- **Location:** Commercial gym — **University rec center**
- **Available equipment:** Standard gym equipment including **barbells, dumbbells, machines** (as reported)

### Constraints
- **[ACTIVE]** Right knee **ACL reconstruction**, **~9 months post-op** and still doing PT (since **~2025-05**, exact surgery date not provided; status confirmed **2026-02-27**).  
  - Notes: nervous about re-injury; training should align with PT guidance and progressive return-to-sport criteria.

### Preferences
- Likes/dislikes: Not specified  
- Training style preferences: Not specified  
- Communication style preferences: Not specified  
- Focus areas: Return-to-sport strength + stability, confidence-building progression

---

## METRICS
*(No performance metrics provided yet — establish baselines at next check-in.)*

### Strength (as of 2026-02-27)
- No current lift numbers reported.
- Baselines to collect (examples): split squat, leg press, RDL, hamstring curl strength, calf raises, core stability, upper-body basics.

### Endurance (as of 2026-02-27)
- No cardio/running metrics reported.
- Baselines to collect (if relevant to soccer): bike intervals tolerance, jog/run tolerance, change-of-direction readiness (only when cleared).

### Movement Quality (as of 2026-02-27)
- **Post-ACL status:** still in PT; readiness measures not provided.
- Baselines to collect (as appropriate/cleared): pain/swelling response, knee ROM, single-leg squat quality, hop/landing mechanics, symmetry measures used by PT.

### Body Composition (as of 2026-02-27)
- Weight / body comp not provided.

---

## LOG

## 2026-02-27 — Profile created/updated (Return-to-soccer post ACL)
- Documented goal: performance-focused return to soccer; rebuild leg strength and confidence post-op.
- Training context added: commercial gym (university rec center), standard equipment; desired frequency **4x/week**.
- Constraints added: **[ACTIVE]** right ACL reconstruction, ~9 months post-op, still in PT; nervous about re-injury.
- Metrics: none provided yet; baseline testing/measure collection needed (aligned with PT clearance).</Profile>

reference.response:
## Header
- **Program name:** Return-to-Soccer Rebuild (Post-ACL) — Strength → Control → Power
- **Program Owner:** GymText
- **User:** Alex Rivera
- **Goal:** Return to soccer performance after right ACL reconstruction (strength, single-leg stability, deceleration/cutting readiness, confidence)
- **Duration:** Ongoing with phase cycling (12-week initial build: 4 + 4 + 4)

---

## Program Philosophy
Alex is a beginner lifter rebuilding capacity ~9 months post-right ACL reconstruction while still in PT and managing fear of re-injury. This program uses a **4-day split** that repeats stable movement patterns each week (for skill + confidence), emphasizing **single-leg strength, hamstring/posterior-chain development, landing mechanics, and progressive knee loading**—all key for return-to-sport. Exercise selection stays **PT-friendly and gym-accessible** (machines/cables/dumbbells first, barbell later as tolerated) and includes explicit “why” notes for tissue tolerance and knee control. Progression is primarily **RPE-based double progression** (add reps first, then load) with planned deloads and **symptom rules** (pain/swelling/instability) to keep rehab aligned and sustainable.

**Non-negotiables / constraints addressed**
- **ACL status + PT:** Jumping/cutting work is **gated by PT clearance** and symptom response; strength work supports PT goals rather than competing with them.
- **Fear reduction:** Uses measurable progression (reps/load/tempo/ROM) and repeated exposures to the same patterns.
- **Beginner complexity:** Simple, repeatable sessions; limited “maxing,” lots of quality reps.

---

# Phase 1 (Weeks 1–4): Foundation — Tissue Capacity + Single-Leg Control
**Intent:** Build tolerance, symmetry, and confidence through controlled strength (tempo/isometrics), machine stability, and low-skill conditioning.

## Weekly Pattern (4 sessions/week)

### Day 1 — Lower A: Knee-Dominant Strength + Trunk
- **Focus:** Quad capacity, knee-over-toe tolerance (within comfort), pelvic control
- **Volume:** ~14–18 working sets
- **Leg Press (feet mid stance, controlled depth):** 4 × 8–12 @ RPE 6–7  
  *Stable way to load quads and gradually rebuild confidence through ROM.*
- **Rear-Foot Elevated Split Squat (DBs, optional light support):** 3 × 8–10/side @ RPE 6–7  
  *Single-leg strength + knee tracking; great “return-to-sport” staple.*
- **Leg Extension (slow eccentric):** 2–3 × 12–15 @ RPE 7  
  *Direct quad work often needed post-ACL; keep motion smooth and pain-free.*
- **Standing Calf Raise:** 3 × 10–15 @ RPE 7  
  *Calf capacity supports deceleration and repeated efforts in soccer.*
- **Pallof Press (cable/band):** 2–3 × 10–12/side (controlled)  
  *Anti-rotation control for cutting mechanics later.*

### Day 2 — Upper A: Push/Pull Base + Shoulder Health
- **Focus:** General strength (supports athleticism + training balance)
- **Volume:** ~14–18 working sets
- **DB Bench Press:** 3 × 8–12 @ RPE 6–7
- **Chest-Supported Row (machine/DB):** 3 × 8–12 @ RPE 7
- **Lat Pulldown (neutral grip if available):** 3 × 10–12 @ RPE 7
- **Incline DB Press or Machine Press:** 2–3 × 10–12 @ RPE 7
- **Cable Face Pull:** 2 × 12–15 @ RPE 7  
  *Scapular control for healthy pressing/pulling volume.*
- **Farmer Carry:** 3 × 30–45 sec (moderate)  
  *Trunk stiffness + grip; “athlete simple.”*

### Day 3 — Lower B: Hinge + Hamstrings + Knee Control
- **Focus:** Posterior chain and hamstrings (critical for ACL protection), plus controlled lateral patterns
- **Volume:** ~14–18 working sets
- **Romanian Deadlift (DB or barbell):** 4 × 6–10 @ RPE 6–7  
  *Hamstring + hip strength; strong transfer to sprinting mechanics later.*
- **Seated or Lying Hamstring Curl:** 3 × 10–15 @ RPE 7–8  
  *Direct hamstring strength is a major ACL re-injury risk reducer.*
- **Step-Down (low box, slow tempo):** 3 × 6–10/side @ RPE 6–7  
  *Eccentric control + knee tracking for deceleration prep.*
- **Cable Hip Abduction or Side-Lying Hip Abduction:** 2–3 × 12–15/side @ RPE 7  
  *Glute med for single-leg stability and cutting readiness.*
- **Dead Bug (or Swiss ball dead bug):** 2–3 × 6–10/side (slow)

### Day 4 — Athletic Foundation: Bike Intervals + Landing Mechanics (Low Level) + Mobility
- **Focus:** Conditioning without knee irritation; basic landing positions; confidence
- **Volume:** ~8–12 strength sets + conditioning
- **Bike Intervals:** 8–12 rounds of 30 sec “hard but smooth” / 60 sec easy (RPE 7–8 on hard)  
  *Soccer-like energy demands without cutting/impact.*
- **Snap-Down to Stick (bodyweight):** 3 × 3–5 reps (perfect mechanics)  
  *Teaches decel/landing positions—only if pain-free and PT-approved.*
- **Lateral Step-Up (low box):** 3 × 8/side @ RPE 6–7  
  *Intro to frontal-plane strength without high speed.*
- **Sled Push (light/moderate, if available):** 6 × 15–25 m (steady pace)  
  *Quad drive with low joint soreness; very ACL-friendly when controlled.*
- **Mobility/Range (as needed):** 8–12 min (ankle DF, hip flexors, gentle knee ROM)

---

## Progression Strategy (Phase 1)
- **Double progression (main lifts):** Stay in the rep range (e.g., 8–12). When you hit the **top end for all sets** at the target RPE, add **2–5% load** next week and restart mid-range.
- **RPE targets:** Mostly **RPE 6–7** (leave 3–4 reps in reserve). Goal is high-quality reps and confidence.
- **Symptom rules (important for ACL):**
  - Pain during: keep ≤ **3/10**, no sharp/unstable feelings.
  - Next-day response: if **swelling increases** or pain >3/10 persists, reduce next session’s lower-body volume by ~30–40% and keep ROM conservative.
- **Deload:** **Week 4** reduce lower-body working sets by ~40–50%, keep load moderate (RPE ~6).
- **Transition to Phase 2 when:** Lower sessions feel stable for 2 straight weeks, no swelling spikes, and split squat/step-down quality is improving (knee tracks cleanly, control is consistent).

---

# Phase 2 (Weeks 5–8): Build — Strength Emphasis + Eccentric Deceleration Prep
**Intent:** Increase strength (especially quads/hamstrings), introduce more deliberate eccentric work and slightly heavier loading while maintaining excellent knee control.

## Weekly Pattern (4 sessions/week)

### Day 1 — Lower A (Strength): Press + Split Squat + Extension
- **Focus:** Heavier knee-dominant strength
- **Volume:** ~14–18 sets
- **Leg Press:** 5 × 5–8 @ RPE 7–8  
- **Rear-Foot Elevated Split Squat:** 4 × 6–8/side @ RPE 7  
- **Leg Extension (1–2 sec squeeze):** 3 × 10–12 @ RPE 8  
- **Calf Raise:** 3 × 8–12 @ RPE 8  
- **Pallof Press or Cable Chop:** 3 × 8–12/side

### Day 2 — Upper A (Strength)
- **Focus:** Heavier presses/rows for general strength
- **Volume:** ~14–18 sets
- **Bench Press (barbell if comfortable, otherwise DB):** 4 × 4–6 @ RPE 7–8
- **Row (chest-supported or cable):** 4 × 6–8 @ RPE 7–8
- **Lat Pulldown / Assisted Pull-Up:** 3 × 6–10 @ RPE 7–8
- **DB Overhead Press (seated):** 2–3 × 8–10 @ RPE 7
- **Face Pull:** 2 × 12–15

### Day 3 — Lower B (Strength): Hinge + Hamstrings + Lateral Control
- **Focus:** Posterior chain strength + frontal-plane robustness
- **Volume:** ~14–18 sets
- **RDL (barbell or DB):** 5 × 4–8 @ RPE 7–8  
- **Hamstring Curl:** 4 × 8–12 @ RPE 8  
- **Step-Down (slightly higher box if tolerated):** 3 × 6–8/side @ RPE 7  
  *Eccentric control = deceleration foundation.*
- **Copenhagen Plank (short lever to start):** 2–3 × 15–25 sec/side  
  *Adductor strength helps with cutting mechanics and groin resilience.*
- **Dead Bug / Plank Variation:** 2–3 sets

### Day 4 — Athletic Build: Conditioning + Low-Amplitude Plyo (Gated)
- **Focus:** Elastic prep + repeat-effort conditioning
- **Volume:** ~6–10 plyo sets + conditioning
- **Bike or Row Intervals:** 10–15 rounds of 20 sec hard / 70 sec easy (hard = RPE 8)  
- **Pogo Hops (double-leg, low amplitude):** 3 × 10–20 sec  
  *Only if cleared and symptom-free; builds ankle stiffness and landing rhythm.*
- **Box Drop to Stick (very low box):** 3 × 3–5  
  *Decel exposure; control > height.*
- **Sled Push or Sled Drag (moderate):** 8 × 15–25 m
- **Mobility/soft tissue:** 8–12 min

---

## Progression Strategy (Phase 2)
- **Main lifts (leg press, split squat, RDL):** Add load in small jumps (2–5%) when all sets land at the top of the rep range with **RPE ≤8** and pristine technique.
- **Eccentric emphasis:** If form is solid, add **tempo** (e.g., 3 sec down) rather than rushing to heavier weight.
- **Deload:** **Week 8** reduce volume 40–50%, keep intensity moderate (RPE 6–7).
- **Transition to Phase 3 when:** You can perform step-downs and split squats with consistent knee tracking and confidence, and PT has no concerns with introducing higher-rate force/low-level plyometrics.

---

# Phase 3 (Weeks 9–12): Return-to-Sport Bridge — Power, Reactive Strength, Controlled COD (PT-Cleared)
**Intent:** Keep strength progressing while layering in more soccer-specific demands: reactive contacts, deceleration, and gradual change-of-direction (COD). **If PT has not cleared plyo/COD, keep Day 4 as conditioning + strength accessories only.**

## Weekly Pattern (4 sessions/week)

### Day 1 — Lower A: Strength Maintenance + Power Primer
- **Focus:** Keep heavy strength; add low-volume power
- **Volume:** ~12–16 sets + brief power
- **Trap Bar Deadlift (or RDL if trap bar unavailable):** 4 × 3–5 @ RPE 7–8  
  *High-force output with typically knee-friendly mechanics.*
- **Leg Press:** 3 × 6–10 @ RPE 7–8
- **Split Squat:** 3 × 6–8/side @ RPE 7–8
- **Optional Power (PT-cleared): Med Ball Scoop Toss or Slams:** 4 × 3–5 (explosive)

### Day 2 — Upper (Strength/Hypertrophy Mix)
- **Focus:** Full upper support; keep progression
- **Volume:** ~14–18 sets
- **Bench Press or DB Bench:** 4 × 4–8 @ RPE 7–8
- **Row:** 4 × 6–10 @ RPE 7–8
- **Pulldown/Pull-Up:** 3 × 6–10 @ RPE 7–8
- **Accessory superset (optional):** curls + triceps 2–3 × 10–15

### Day 3 — Lower B: Hamstring Priority + Single-Leg Elastic Prep
- **Focus:** Hamstrings + single-leg robustness
- **Volume:** ~14–18 sets
- **Hamstring Curl (heavy-ish):** 5 × 6–10 @ RPE 8  
- **RDL:** 3 × 6–10 @ RPE 7–8
- **Lateral Lunge (short ROM initially):** 3 × 6–8/side @ RPE 6–7  
  *Builds frontal-plane capacity needed for cutting and shuffling.*
- **Calf Raise:** 3 × 8–12 @ RPE 8
- **Side Plank or Copenhagen (progress lever as tolerated):** 2–3 sets

### Day 4 — Field/Gym Return-to-Sport Session (Gated)
- **Focus:** Plyometrics → decel → COD progressions (low volume, high quality)
- **Volume:** ~30–45 min total, low-to-moderate contacts
- **Warm-up (10 min):** bike 5 min + dynamic (leg swings, marching, ankle hops practice)
- **Plyo (PT-cleared):**
  - **Pogo hops:** 3 × 15–25 sec
  - **Countermovement jump to stick:** 4 × 2–4 (stick landing 2 sec)
- **Deceleration mechanics:**
  - **Run-in decel to stop (submax):** 6–10 reps over 10–15 m (walk back)  
    *Teaches braking; progress speed before adding complexity.*
- **COD (only when cleared):**
  - **45° cut at controlled speed:** 6–8 reps/side  
  - Progress later to **90°** and then reactive cues (not in this initial 4-week block)
- **Conditioning finisher (optional):** 6–10 × 15 sec hard / 45 sec easy on bike

---

## Progression Strategy (Phase 3)
- **Strength:** Hold intensity steady (RPE 7–8) and progress slowly; the “sport” day adds stress.
- **Plyo/COD:** Progress **one variable at a time** (contacts → speed → angle → reaction). Stop sets if landing quality drops.
- **Deload:** Week 12 reduce lower strength volume ~40% and cut plyo/COD volume in half.
- **Exit criteria:** Confident landings, no swelling spikes, and PT-approved progression toward higher-speed, reactive, and soccer-integrated work.

---

## Phase Cycling
**Order:** Phase 1 (Foundation) → Phase 2 (Build) → Phase 3 (Return-to-Sport Bridge) → repeat with higher baselines.

**How cycling works (ongoing):**
- If PT introduces new return-to-sport tasks (running, hops, COD), Phase 3 becomes the main focus while keeping 2 lower strength days.
- If symptoms flare (swelling/pain/instability), cycle back to **Phase 1–2 loading strategies** (more machines, slower tempos, less plyo/COD) for 2–4 weeks.
- Every 12 weeks: take a lighter week, re-check baselines, then restart the cycle with slightly higher starting loads/ROM/complexity.

**Suggested readiness markers to track (align with PT):**
- Next-day knee response (swelling/stiffness), confidence rating (0–10)
- Split squat quality (knee tracking, depth, control)
- Hamstring curl strength progress and tolerance
- Landing “stick” quality consistency (Phase 2–3, if cleared)

---

## Modification History
- **2026-02-27:** Initial program created for post-ACL return-to-soccer goal; 3-phase progression with PT-gated plyo/COD and symptom-based autoregulation.
